actual_manual_intervention_hours_per_wee.rationale: Plan threshold is 2.0 hr/week; base 1.8 sits just inside the gate. Anchored on Issue 2 — 10% material variance reduces probability of meeting the 2-hour goal by 50-70%; high-side reflects Risk 2 used-machinery integration drift past the cap. Spread is asymmetric: irreducible operator touchpoints floor near 0.5, while debugging excursions can run hours.

actual_onsite_expert_cost_usd.rationale: Plan floor is $15k for 1 week on-site; cap is $25k. Base 18k centred between Issue-3 floor and Premortem statement that on-site PLC integration can be contracted for under $30k. Low 12k from the Issue-3 emergency on-site week band ($7k-$12k); high 30k from the Premortem $30k ceiling for the first engagement.

opc_ua_middleware_cost_usd.rationale: Base at Q3 inferred $50k allocation. High 75k from the Premortem assumption that OPC UA middleware development cost must not exceed $75,000. Low 35k reflects competitive specialist quotes when scope is well-defined.

wire_bender_midpoint_usd.rationale: Stated $20k-$40k allocation range from Selected Scenario and Expert Criticism. Base at midpoint; bounds at the explicit endpoints. Hard ceiling reflects the plan's stated CAPEX envelope for the bender.

packing_machine_midpoint_usd.rationale: Stated $10k-$30k allocation range from Expert Criticism. Base at midpoint; bounds at the explicit endpoints.

permits_and_safety_cost_usd.rationale: Source does not name a separate allocation. Base 18k anchored to Risk-1 cost-increase band ($10k-$20k) for regulatory delay; high 35k covers third-party safety-interlock retrofit flagged by Expert Criticism (OSHA pre-engineering). Low 8k assumes clean permit path on the existing industrial zone.

rigging_and_install_cost_usd.rationale: Source does not name a rigging line item. Base anchored to typical industrial rigging plus install for two machines into a 4,000 sq ft cell. High-side asymmetry reflects Expert Criticism flagging Phase 3/5 integration labour as underbudgeted.

internal_developer_cost_usd.rationale: Source does not state developer-week allocation. Base assumes loaded FTE rate around $2k/week for 30-40 internal developer-weeks across the 6-phase plan. High-side reflects Risk 4 (single-developer bottleneck) extending the timeline; low-side reflects compressed scope.
